suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document a Windows-safe fallback for stopping the local dev server in web-feature-worker, avoiding PowerShell variable interpolation/quoting patterns that fail under the exec wrapper.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The history-export-consistency review observed the worker hitting a cleanup failure (`5 was unexpected at this time.`) while trying to stop the dev server with the manifest-style command on Windows.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify in `web-feature-worker` that persisted local-state fixes in the monolithic dashboard may be adequately validated with targeted post-implementation regression tests, and that storage-backed persistence fixes can use deterministic storage-event/helper coverage plus dev-server evidence when browser automation is unavailable.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both `history-filters-preferences-and-reset` and `history-saved-lookups-rehydration-fix` reviews noted the current skill guidance is slightly too rigid around failing-tests-first/browser fallback framing for persisted-state changes on this Windows host.

rejectedObservations.observation: Document a wrapper-safe Windows stop fallback in shared state knowledge.
rejectedObservations.reason: recommendation overlaps with the retained skills guidance update and does not require an additive factual services/library change in this pass.
